name: redb
description: Simple, portable, high-performance, ACID embedded key-value store written in pure Rust, inspired by LMDB

description.value: Simple, portable, high-performance, ACID embedded key-value store written in pure Rust, inspired by LMDB
description.comment: Designed as a pure Rust alternative to LMDB with type-safe API

implementation.comment: 100% pure Rust with no C dependencies except libc for file locking

schema-model.comment: Uses Rust generics with TableDefinition<K, V> for compile-time type safety

transactions.comment: ACID-compliant with configurable durability modes and savepoints

range-queries.comment: Ordered B-tree iteration with range() method

secondary-indexes.comment: Key-value store only, no built-in secondary index support

in-memory-mode.comment: Requires file backing; can use tempfile for temporary databases

storage-architecture.comment: Uses copy-on-write with dual database headers for atomic commits

max-db-size.comment: v2 format ~32TB limit for savepoints; v3 supports larger databases

pure-rust.comment: Pure Rust with no C dependencies except libc

async-support.comment: Blocking I/O only; issue #30 tracks async read interface

api-safety.comment: Public API is completely safe Rust

idiomatic-api.comment: Type-safe BTreeMap-like API with TableDefinition generics, very Rust-idiomatic

type-safety.comment: Compile-time type safety via TableDefinition<K, V> generics

serde-integration.comment: No built-in serde; examples show bincode pattern for custom serialization

wasm-compatible.comment: WASI support via wasm32-wasip1-threads target, not browser WASM

acid-compliant.comment: Full ACID with configurable durability modes

crash-recovery.comment: Automatic recovery with two-phase commit option for faster recovery

multi-process.comment: v3.0.0+ allows multiple read-only processes via ReadOnlyDatabase with file locks

write-ahead-log.comment: Uses copy-on-write with dual database headers instead of WAL

maturity.comment: 1.0 stable released June 2023, file format is stable

ios.comment: Not explicitly documented; pure Rust suggests possible but unverified

android.comment: Not explicitly documented; pure Rust suggests possible but unverified

use-cases.comment: Best for single-writer, multiple-reader scenarios with type-safe requirements

documentation-quality.comment: 68.46% coverage, good API docs, examples, design document

bus-factor.comment: Christopher Berner is primary author with 30 contributors

hot-backup.comment: Cannot safely copy database file during writes. Must use begin_read() and manually iterate/export data within a transaction for consistent backup.

incremental-backup.comment: No incremental backup or change tracking support. No WAL to track changes.

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Savepoints exist but are for transaction rollback only, not arbitrary point-in-time recovery. No WAL retention for PITR.

online-compaction.comment: compact() requires exclusive access (no active transactions). Returns TransactionInProgress error if any readers exist.

repair-tools.comment: check_integrity() verifies and repairs database. RepairSession provides progress tracking. Returns Ok(true) if valid, Ok(false) if repaired.

export-format.comment: No built-in export. Issue #411 requested dump/restore but was declined. Users must manually iterate tables via iter()/range().
